name: Buchla 242t Programmable Pulser
description: Licensed Eurorack reissue of the Buchla 200-series 242 pulser. 4×12 LED matrix replaces the original pin matrix — three independent pulse patterns (A/B/C) clocked by an internal period from 2ms to 10s+ per stage, plus alternating, all-pulse and ramp outputs.

controls.name: PERIOD offset
controls.description: Right knob · sets base stage period from ~2ms (audio rate) to ~10 seconds or longer · labeled .002 to 10/OFF
controls.detail: CW past '10' reaches OFF, stopping the internal clock

controls.name: PERIOD attenuverter
controls.description: Left knob · bipolar CV attenuator on period modulation · adds or subtracts from offset knob

controls.name: Vertical-row encoder
controls.description: Lower-left knob · scrolls cursor through rows n / A / B / C to select which row is edited
controls.detail: Combined with horizontal encoder to address a matrix cell

controls.name: Horizontal-row encoder
controls.description: Lower-right knob · scrolls cursor through stages 1-12
controls.detail: Combined with vertical encoder to address a matrix cell

controls.name: on/off button
controls.description: Toggles the currently-selected matrix cell on/off · long press clears the whole matrix
controls.detail: 'n' row selects stage count; A/B/C rows set pulse pattern per output

controls.name: start button
controls.description: Manually starts the internal clock and matrix playback
controls.detail: Also triggered by start input on INPUTS jack

controls.name: single pulse button
controls.description: Emits one manual pulse without starting the clock
controls.detail: Useful for step-advance testing or filling in patterns live

controls.name: stop button
controls.detail: Also triggered by stop input on INPUTS jack

controls.name: Pulse-length jumper (rear)
controls.description: Short = pulser's internal brief width or CLK IN width · Long = fixed 1ms · factory default Long for Eurorack compatibility
controls.detail: Back of module; requires removing from rack

inputs.name: start IN
inputs.description: Pulse input · rising edge starts the internal clock

inputs.name: stop IN
inputs.description: Pulse input · rising edge stops the internal clock

inputs.name: PERIOD CV
inputs.description: Modulation CV scaled by the PERIOD attenuverter · sweeps stage period across the full range

inputs.name: CLK IN
inputs.description: External clock input · replaces internal clock for stage advance · each rising edge advances one stage

inputs.name: RST IN
inputs.description: External reset · returns playback to the end stage / wraps the pattern

outputs.name: ALL OUT
outputs.description: Continuous pulse train at the internal (or CLK IN) rate regardless of matrix content · direct tap on the pulser

outputs.name: MATRIX A
outputs.description: Pulses programmed in row A of the matrix · active on stages 1 to 'n'

outputs.name: MATRIX B
outputs.description: Pulses programmed in row B of the matrix

outputs.name: MATRIX C
outputs.description: Pulses programmed in row C of the matrix

outputs.name: ALTER (×2)
outputs.description: Two jacks emitting alternating pulses from the pulser · pulse N goes to one, N+1 to the other · ping-pong rhythm pairs

outputs.name: Ramp OUT (gray)
outputs.description: Continuous falling ramp synced to the pulser · spans one stage period · ideal for driving a 292t LPG strike directly

behaviors.name: Three-row independent matrix
behaviors.trigger: program cells in rows A, B, C
behaviors.description: Rows A, B, C each output their own pulse pattern through their own jack. The 'n' row at the top selects how many of the 12 stages are active per row — patterns of different lengths can run simultaneously against each other, producing polymeter (e.g. A=5, B=7, C=12).

behaviors.name: Internal vs external clock
behaviors.description: External clock on CLK IN takes over stage advance for MATRIX A/B/C and the ALTER outputs. However, ALL OUT and the ramp output remain driven by the internal clock period — so external clocking does not silence the internal pulse/ramp.

behaviors.name: Ramp for LPG strike
behaviors.trigger: patch gray Ramp OUT to 292t-style LPG
behaviors.description: The gray ramp output is specifically designed to open a Buchla 292t (or any vactrol LPG) directly without needing an envelope generator. The ramp's falling slope acts as the decay envelope; period controls the LPG's decay time.

behaviors.name: Ping-pong via ALTER pair
behaviors.trigger: patch both ALTER outputs to paired destinations
behaviors.description: The two ALTER jacks split every-other pulse — pulse 1 to jack 1, pulse 2 to jack 2, pulse 3 to jack 1 again, etc. Use for stereo ping-pong triggering of two drum voices, or left/right envelopes, without any logic module.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate operation
behaviors.description: Period range extends to ~2ms, which is audio-rate (~500Hz fundamental). The ALL OUT and matrix outputs become clock-like square waves suitable as audio sources for PWM-style patches or unusual drones.

behaviors.name: OFF position stops internal clock
behaviors.description: Panel label .002-10-OFF: past the '10-seconds' end the knob hits OFF, halting the internal clock. Lets you freeze playback with one gesture without touching the stop button or a stop pulse.

behaviors.name: Long-press matrix clear
behaviors.description: Long press of the on/off button clears all programmed pulse cells. Shorter press only toggles the currently-selected cell. Use for fresh starts during live programming without navigating to each cell.

behaviors.name: Pulse-length jumper trade-off
behaviors.description: Default 'Long' = 1ms fixed width, compatible with envelope-generator triggers. 'Short' uses the pulser's native (much briefer) width or the CLK IN pulse width directly — useful when clocking fast digital destinations that might miss a 1ms pulse-train edge.

patchIdeas.name: Three-part polymetric drum pattern
patchIdeas.patch: program A=kick pattern (length 4), B=hats (length 7), C=snare (length 12) · same internal clock · each MATRIX output to its drum voice · classic Buchla-style polymetric groove that never repeats the same way for 84 bars
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* n(A)=4, n(B)=7, n(C)=12
* A: 1,3 · B: 1,4,6 · C: 5,9,11
* PERIOD: ~125ms (120 BPM sixteenths)

Kick:
Hats:
Snare:

- 242t (MATRIX A) g> Kick (Trig)
- 242t (MATRIX B) g> Hats (Trig)
- 242t (MATRIX C) g> Snare (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Direct LPG strike via ramp
patchIdeas.patch: ramp OUT to 292t (or any vactrol LPG) CV · matrix A triggers the 259t/258t voice · each stage fires a self-enveloping note with no extra envelope module needed · PERIOD knob shapes the decay
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,3,5,7
* PERIOD: ~400ms

VCO:
LPG:

- VCO (Out) -> LPG (In)
- 242t (Ramp OUT) >> LPG (CV) // self-striking ramp
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> VCO (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Ping-pong stereo envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: ALL OUT drives a steady pulse · wait, use ALTER 1 → L envelope, ALTER 2 → R envelope · two mono voices get alternating triggers · result = stereo ping-pong rhythmic pattern without any logic or switch module
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* PERIOD: 250ms

Env L:
Env R:
Voice L:
Voice R:

- 242t (ALTER 1) t> Env L (Trig)
- 242t (ALTER 2) t> Env R (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Externally-clocked pattern with kept ramp
patchIdeas.patch: master clock to CLK IN · matrix runs in sync to rack tempo · but ALL OUT and ramp keep running on the internal PERIOD — use the ramp for free-running modulation that does not sync to clock while the matrix stays tight
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* Internal PERIOD: 1.3s (modulation rate)

Master Clock:
Filter:
VCO:

- Master Clock (Out) c> 242t (CLK IN)
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> VCO (Trig) // synced rhythm
- 242t (Ramp OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff) // free-running mod

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate matrix drone
patchIdeas.patch: PERIOD at full CCW (~2ms) · matrix A and B programmed with different patterns · outputs become audio-rate square-wave-like tones whose harmonic content depends on the on/off pattern · through a filter for subtractive-style lead
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* PERIOD: fully CCW (~2ms)
* A: 1,2,4,6,8 · B: 1,3,5,9

Filter:
VCA:

- 242t (MATRIX A) -> Filter (In)
- 242t (MATRIX B) -> Filter (In) // sum via in
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: CV-swept tempo
patchIdeas.patch: slow LFO to PERIOD CV input · attenuverter sets sweep depth · matrix keeps pattern, but period swells and contracts over time · great for accelerando/decelerando gestures with hands-free
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,4,7,10
* PERIOD attenuverter: 2 o'clock

LFO:
Drum:

- LFO (Out) >> 242t (PERIOD CV) // tempo breathes
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Reset to cut pattern short
patchIdeas.patch: program A=12 stages but send a reset pulse at stage 8 via external source · matrix wraps early · combine with CLK IN for effective variable pattern length driven by external gate logic
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1-12 all on
* Via CLK IN

Master Clock:
Reset Trig:
Drum:

- Master Clock (Out) c> 242t (CLK IN)
- Reset Trig (Out) t> 242t (RST IN) // cut pattern early
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Start/stop performance transport
patchIdeas.patch: use start and stop buttons (or CV) as live transport · hold stop during breakdowns, punch start to re-enter · single-pulse button fills with one-off hits during pauses · patterns resume from where they stopped
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,3,5,7,9,11

Footswitch:
Drum:

- Footswitch (Start) g> 242t (start IN)
- Footswitch (Stop) g> 242t (stop IN)
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Generative melody via ALL OUT + quantizer
patchIdeas.patch: ALL OUT steady clock · matrix A triggers a random/S&H voice · B triggers a second voice · C triggers an accent · three-voice generative line with polyrhythm between them
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* n(A)=5, n(B)=7, n(C)=3

S&H:
VCO:
Noise:
Quantizer:
Drum:
Accent Drum:

- Noise (Out) >> S&H (In)
- 242t (ALL OUT) c> S&H (Trig)
- S&H (Out) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- 242t (MATRIX A) g> VCO (Gate)
- 242t (MATRIX B) t> Drum (Trig)
- 242t (MATRIX C) t> Accent Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Ratchet fills via audio-rate + gate
patchIdeas.patch: main slow pattern on MATRIX A · use a VCA in its signal path whose CV comes from a gate source · when gate opens, let a second 242t at audio-rate pass through — creates ratcheting fills on demand · single module + VCA trick
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,3,5,7 · PERIOD: 300ms

Fast 242t:
VCA:
Mixer:
Drum:

- 242t (MATRIX A) t> Drum (Trig) // main pulse
- Fast 242t (ALL OUT) -> VCA (In) // audio-rate
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In 2) // ratchet when gated
- 242t (MATRIX A) g> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Matrix ALL + A/B as clock divider
patchIdeas.patch: ALL OUT = master clock · program A to fire on stages 1,5,9 (÷4) · B on 1,7 (÷6) · C on 1 (÷12) · single module emits master clock plus three integer divisions for tempo-locked modulations
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,5,9 · B: 1,7 · C: 1

Envelope:
LFO Trigger:
Filter:

- 242t (ALL OUT) c> Envelope (Trig) // master
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> LFO Trigger (Reset) // ÷4
- 242t (MATRIX B) >> Filter (Cutoff) // ÷6 slow accent
- 242t (MATRIX C) t> LFO Trigger (Reset) // ÷12 downbeat

patchIdeas.name: Self-patched tempo nudge
patchIdeas.patch: ALTER 1 into PERIOD CV with attenuverter mostly CCW · every other stage gives a tiny tempo kick · feels like swing or humanized drift baked into the pulser itself
patchIdeas.diagram:
242t:
* A: 1,3,5,7,9,11
* PERIOD attenuverter: 10 o'clock (small)

Drum:

- 242t (ALTER 1) >> 242t (PERIOD CV) // subtle swing
- 242t (MATRIX A) t> Drum (Trig)
